Who I Am
About Me

I'm a Mechanical Engineering Technology student at Purdue's Polytechnic Institute, graduating May 2028 with a minor in Design and Innovation. I combine engineering fundamentals with product instincts — designing things not just to exist, but to perform.

My background is unusually broad: I've competed at national Taekwondo tournaments, earned my BSA Eagle Scout, led fundraising as a student treasurer, and shipped real mechanical designs for solar racing cars and accessibility engineering projects.

I'm fluent in the full design-to-fabrication pipeline — from ideation in CAD to GD&T-compliant drawings ready for CNC machining. I pick up tools fast, work well in teams, and care deeply about elegant solutions.

Crown Liquors

Retail Associate · Hyattsville, MD

Built Things
Featured Projects
Purdue EPICS · Caterpillar Inc.
Bubble Tube Sensory System

Contributed to the design, fabrication, and validation of a sensory Bubble Tube system intended for children. As Project Partner Liaison and a member of the box-design team, I helped improve enclosure usability, mobility, durability, and sealing performance through repeated testing and redesign over a 14-week engineering cycle.

Iterative Design Fabrication Leak Testing Design Reviews Accessibility
View Engineering Notebook ↗ View Role Summary
Weeks 1–3
Project onboarding, planning, enclosure repair, sanding, and painting.
Weeks 4–7
Water-pressure testing and early sealing iterations using tape and Mega Loc.
Weeks 8–12
Added latches and wheels, repaired the tube, installed a clamp, and cut new PVC doors.
Weeks 13–14
Completed finishing work, final seal validation, and the final engineering design review.
3.5V
Team Project · 5 Members
3D Printed Alternator Pulley Generator

Led CAD modeling for a low-cost pulley-based electricity generator using SolidWorks and Siemens NX. Prototyped and tested a PLA-based system with magnets and copper wire — achieving a 3.5V output on a shoestring $50 budget. Demonstrates end-to-end engineering from design to functional prototype.

SolidWorks Siemens NX 3D Printing (PLA) Electromagnetics Prototyping
🏎️
Purdue Solar Racing
Regenerative Braking Paddle

Designed and iterated through 3+ cycles in Autodesk Inventor. Produced GD&T-compliant drawings for CNC machining and system integration into a solar racing vehicle.

Inventor GD&T CNC Machining
Personal Project
Butterfly Valve System

Designed a two-way butterfly valve system in Siemens NX — a full assembly including elbow pipe sections, valve plate, shaft, fasteners, and a Woodruff key. Fully constrained assembly with GD&T-compliant modeling.

CAD Portfolio
Single-Piston Engine

Full engine assembly modeled in CAD including all moving components, toleranced for manufacturability.

Siemens NXAssembly
CAD Portfolio
Arbor Press

Full 15-component arbor press assembly designed in Inventor — includes cast iron frame, pinion shaft, RAM, GIB plates, collar, and all fasteners. GD&T-compliant engineering drawings produced for all parts.
